steps.explanation: Walking takes a fixed 20 minutes. Taking the bus takes \(w\) minutes of waiting plus 5 minutes of riding, for a total of \(w + 5\) minutes.
Walking is faster than the bus when the walking time is less than the bus total time: \(20 < w + 5\), which rearranges to \(w + 5 > 20\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: \(w - 5 < 20\) simplifies to \(w < 25\). This is almost always true within the 0–30 range and does not identify when walking is faster. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(w - 5 > 20\) simplifies to \(w > 25\), meaning the bus total would exceed 30 minutes, which is near the edge of the wait range. This does not correctly represent the comparison. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(w + 5 < 20\) simplifies to \(w < 15\). This says the bus is faster than walking, the opposite of what is asked. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(w + 5 > 20\) directly states that the total bus time exceeds the 20-minute walk, so walking is faster. That matches choice D — D is correct.
